subject: Welcome to the general election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/welcome-to-the-general-election?e=1acd9bb3d6)
https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1109784619387174&mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Welcome to the General Election

This week, Indiana held its primary election. We now officially have most of our nominees who will represent our party and secure more victories up and down the ballot this November.

Welcome to the general election!

And in less than two weeks, I’m excited that we’ll have our Indiana Republican State Convention (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=%5bUNIQID%5d&mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , which will finalize our ticket by officially nominating our candidates for Lt. Governor and Attorney General. While I’m still disappointed we won’t all be together, I’m looking forward to putting our Party of Purpose on full display to a statewide audience through a TV broadcast and livestream.

Here at your Indiana Republican Party, we’re in full preparation mode for holding your state convention. As you know, the live broadcast will be on June 18 at 5:30 p.m. ET/4:30 p.m. CT. It’ll be aired by WISH-TV, and its statewide news partners. It’ll also be livestreamed, and available on our website for viewing after the event.

But new this week, we released a video (https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1109784619387174&mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) for our delegates, explaining in detail how voting will work this year. We don’t want there to be any confusion, so please take a few minutes to watch this video (https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1109784619387174&mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) . It takes you through when you’ll get your ballot in the mail, how rounds of voting will work, and how your votes will be counted.

And also don’t forget, if you’ve been elected or appointed to serve as a delegate, your deadline to pay your delegate fee is Tuesday, June 9. We’ve got all of this information – including the link to pay your delegate fees – updated at Indiana.gop/stateconvention (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

It’s certainly an unusual year, but together, I know our hard work will earn Republicans more wins and more opportunities to continue to lead our state and our communities.

#BackonTrack,
- Chairman Kyle Hupfer
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was
https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/videos/287954505694622/?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

We Don't Have More Time or Lives to Lose

Over the past week, many Hoosiers have joined in the nationwide protests of the death of George Floyd. Governor Holcomb stands with those who are peacefully exercising their first amendment right, while he condemns unnecessary and tragic violence and destruction.

Watch his full remarks on this important topic from Monday here (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/videos/287954505694622/?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

“I implore every Hoosier... to use your breath and your will in efforts to bridge, not divide. Violence in the streets makes progress harder... and sets us back from forming that more perfect union.”

In Indiana, we’ve made progress on supporting Hoosiers from diverse backgrounds, including passing Indiana’s first ever hate crimes law protecting 6.7 million Hoosiers, launching free training for in-demand jobs, and providing resources to address infant mortality in Indiana’s hardest hit areas.

But we’re not done yet. We must create a to-do list, with action steps including:
* Better healthcare access
* Affordable housing
* Educational opportunity
* Job training and placement
* Urban economic development
* Criminal justice reform

"George Floyd’s final words ‘I can’t breathe’ haunts and should forever remind us that every breathe that we take... that we have left should be devoted to making sure what happened to Mr. Floyd never happens again. We must harness, not squander, the energy generated in the aftermath of the Floyd injustice.”

Governor Holcomb is dedicated to creating an inclusive state that works for all Hoosiers and upholds the values of our great country. We have brighter days ahead, but it is going to take all of us working together to get there.

http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]
2020 Indiana Republican State Convention

Congratulations to all delegates to the state convention elected on Tuesday!

Our 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention is now less than two weeks away. For the first time in state history, we’ll be broadcasting our convention LIVE on TV and on the internet. Tune in on Thursday, June 18 at 5:30 p.m. ET, and hear from all of our candidates for attorney general, Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch, and a keynote address from Governor Eric Holcomb.

For more information, including a new video explaining voting procedures, visit Indiana.gop/stateconvention (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .
Congratulations, Primary Winners!

Congratulations to all Hoosier Republicans who threw their hats into the ring to run for office this primary, and congratulations to all of our new Republican nominees. Together, we’ll work hard to secure more Republican victories up and down the ballot the November.

Here’s a list of our statewide and congressional candidates nominated on Tuesday:

https://chiefexecutive.net/2020-best-worst-for-states-business/?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]
Indiana: Best in the Midwest for Business

It’s official (again!). This week, Chief Executive magazine ranked Indiana as having the best business climate in the Midwest and the fifth-best business climate in the nation. With this strong foundation built by our Republican leaders, Indiana is well-positioned to continue recovering from COVID-19 and keep getting back on track!

--> Check out the full ranking (https://chiefexecutive.net/2020-best-worst-for-states-business/?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) here (https://chiefexecutive.net/2020-best-worst-for-states-business/?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) ! <-- (https://chiefexecutive.net/2020-best-worst-for-states-business/?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
We're Bouncing Back

As states and businesses across the country have begun their phased reopening, the economy is beginning to recover! The U.S. economy gained 2.5 million jobs in May and the unemployment rate dropped to 13.3 percent, down from 14.7 percent in April, according to the monthly employment report released Friday by the Bureau of Labor Statistics!

"We're going to be back higher next year than ever before." -President Donald Trump
After these numbers were released, the Dow Jones Industrial Average also rose by almost 800 points in premarket activity! Investors have begun to train their focus on the nation's reopening and positive signs of recovery.

“This recovery begins today. We’re going to do whatever it takes to get the American people through this pandemic.” -Vice President Mike Pence

--> Check out the full article here! <-- (https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/unemployment-rate-falls-13-3-percent-economy-gains-surprise-2-n1223236?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

News You Can Use

Governor Eric Holcomb extends public health emergency to July 4 (https://www.abc57.com/news/governor-holcomb-extends-public-health-emergency-to-july-4?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch promotes second round of broadband funding (https://www.insideindianabusiness.com/story/42210349/broadband-grant-program-totals-dollar100m-in-requests?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Senator Todd Young says relief is on the way for small businesses (https://www.journalgazette.net/article/20200604/WEB/200609952?mc_cid=d061952707&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

Thank you, Charlie Blaettler, Campaign Manager \-------- Subj: AOC just endorsed our campaign! From: Mondaire Jones
Friend, **** **** **** During her run for Congress, Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez famously said that people like her “aren’t supposed to run for office.” I felt that. Growing up poor, black, and gay, I never imagined that someone like me could run for Congress, much less come this close to winning.
But Rep. Ocasio-Cortez has changed that impression for so many people who wonder if there is a place for them in a world filled with so much injustice. We need more people in Congress like her, who understand, through their lived experiences, the urgency of racial justice and economic fairness.
It’s an absolute honor to receive her endorsement in our fight to deliver true justice and equality for the people of New York’s 17th Congressional District and across this nation. I can’t wait to work with Rep. Ocasio-Cortez to pass long overdue bills like Medicare for All, the Green New Deal for Public Housing Act, and the Recognizing Poverty Act.
Like Rep. Ocasio-Cortez, I am funding my race the right way. No corporate PACs. No fossil fuel industry lobbyists. No self-funding. Just people.
**_[That’s why I need you to chip in.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953z0IZfSrbb6JB3qMyNklARg9dV9Ld7IyxIIoJP5KvJv0C8ArP4Tqor7D1qx_uoyvIcxHOx88OVmSmJCiPDHDER3mYzoCEgIlDR3cUEdBL2JtER9DzwN_vfTs3DJmcIhhJp4HJHCL-7QNhUPEiWfi6F7kz8qg52tKVOdPhkOdSacMZtYT17OWs0vz6nxdF6UIDZQgikqJyBJ9h1Czg8x4HwDr14aZ6QrRV6yYz26ugmGAGhSgnoXv3IsZKzKsvru4R4ojR6GFy-YzonlFf1imUbr4XJfAk1-A9kk6KYHa4j8E6tztD5g-R-hvCwT66a5s0KfdEUNHAjNdjvr0OYAFuYM/32d/KivPEheHTza9EsUuoVzbfw/h4/m8pg_PlmTVekIWKXibjQr3uVIwEOZyuuz2ybyoADZ2M)_** **_[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yrc7gJyBcGWoBIRLqbcnCMT0QncI8UZQHy6hjtHS-cEUVB3b5gGoxD2fkV9vlIzpbC4juny89IHW65IRsqOISTydfIArTXtPaSg9yauAtkxUfAxtPCArkU1p71tLg-qyK_i-oaB2X08xZ7mcyWfpEP0LZ9Y697Pw7Z3vX8FTkx0yU2K_V5ftP9SbZ48DfgYdipKHG2aiDUhq-3rFgoMPDgiz24tRHCDGaKBzpC9rHCAaAL5bVoqfoTgAkZOdOtqMarv9yyNXxLHlhAq-JxFrDotdqLD7fbgpOzEiZqSRNyOu9RW4e3arDbJTZUSUEiZx9aEYje9INDub5mbJ0UXdW7/32d/KivPEheHTza9EsUuoVzbfw/h5/U_VrkGgyglXmvznMXqrFv99Bc7L4uXd0e89Z4W8v5Y8)_**

And I need your help. **_[Can you make a contribution today?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953z0IZfSrbb6JB3qMyNklARg9dV9Ld7IyxIIoJP5KvJv0GErjM9g0hdB-goKHjQjS5yx4IHsPimFcurggnV1G39-0sG9ZJWLCJLVdKGwRWKrp_SyBLNS3jTcc8UzmQMkrJO2iak_ttJGjplErMQvbEF1a7kA5XYXLdE7TMvDGA74hZRxiet2nb32p1d3td4N1bClIBZuF0vQIoQAeF-9T85whUUh9fyffQgqqHydctrSatdkQ9E5tPZba3UW5GPXRbtV95CKJgyQQWT1eTJo2QPL070PDFiRBmXaGIjrZzSBAXNnDTBuZywgMXh7NHmOrBXIJLIJpGw0VDpX1D242bPu/32d/KivPEheHTza9EsUuoVzbfw/h7/S-rF3zOAXk5T4Lq1Rz0BCkVP-neEso1x1CB5kaPvaIk)_**

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dave=20Lindstrom?=
subject: We're on the Road to Recovery
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lindstromforsenate.com/were-on-the-road-to-recovery?e=41f8e27552)
---
| |
---
| | Today’s jobs report brought good news for our country. In May, we gained 2.5 million jobs and unemployment fell. This is the single largest monthly jobs growth ever. This was after economists kept predicting soaring unemployment for the month of May. We’re showing that the country is on its way back. This news should not be a surprise to anyone. The American economy was always on track to bounce back – and bounce back quickly. Our business owners and companies want to get back to work and bring back employees. All Americans want to go back to work. The high unemployment that we’ve seen in recent months was due to the Covid-19 pandemic and the closures of businesses nationwide. But due to the unique circumstances of the pandemic and closures, anyone could have foreseen the economy bouncing back just as quickly as it declined. Sadly, some economists wanted to project doomsday and predict another Great Depression. But we’ve proved them wrong. President Trump’s leadership during this time has moved our economy forward. President Trump and his team have been working tirelessly to keep our economy up and running. Republicans in Congress have worked tirelessly to help President Trump with this. I am running for the U.S. Senate because I am a business owner who wants to help grow our economy. President Trump needs more allies in the Senate with private sector experience. Business owners know what is needed to grow that economy far better than career politicians who hopscotch from office to office. While today’s report had good news, we must remember that we still have 13-percent unemployment, so our work is far from over. Though the current report shows that the recovery should continue quickly. There is more we must do, including establishing pro-growth policies, continuing to reform the federal tax code, bringing broadband to rural communities and implementing the new USMCA trade agreement. I am running for the Senate so I can work with President Trump on his America First priorities for We the People!

Dave

P.S. - Please click on the logo below to help us bring our message to all the people of Kansas.

---

clean_body: | | | | | | | Today’s jobs report brought good news for our country. In May, we gained 2.5 million jobs and unemployment fell. This is the single largest monthly jobs growth ever. This was after economists kept predicting soaring unemployment for the month of May. We’re showing that the country is on its way back. This news should not be a surprise to anyone. The American economy was always on track to bounce back – and bounce back quickly. Our business owners and companies want to get back to work and bring back employees. All Americans want to go back to work. The high unemployment that we’ve seen in recent months was due to the Covid-19 pandemic and the closures of businesses nationwide. But due to the unique circumstances of the pandemic and closures, anyone could have foreseen the economy bouncing back just as quickly as it declined. Sadly, some economists wanted to project doomsday and predict another Great Depression. But we’ve proved them wrong. President Trump’s leadership during this time has moved our economy forward. President Trump and his team have been working tirelessly to keep our economy up and running. Republicans in Congress have worked tirelessly to help President Trump with this. I am running for the U.S. Senate because I am a business owner who wants to help grow our economy. President Trump needs more allies in the Senate with private sector experience. Business owners know what is needed to grow that economy far better than career politicians who hopscotch from office to office. While today’s report had good news, we must remember that we still have 13-percent unemployment, so our work is far from over. Though the current report shows that the recovery should continue quickly. There is more we must do, including establishing pro-growth policies, continuing to reform the federal tax code, bringing broadband to rural communities and implementing the new USMCA trade agreement. I am running for the Senate so I can work with President Trump on his America First priorities for We the People! Dave P.S. - Please click on the logo below to help us bring our message to all the people of Kansas. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2020 Lindstrom for Senate, All rights reserved.

| I entered this race when I learned that several NH veterans had been turned away from mental health treatment because the Veteran’s Administration was so far behind on reimbursements that the VA owes the medical community in New Hampshire $134,000,000. While hospitals are laying off employees and veterans are going without care, **Mr. Pappas is too busy trying to impeach Donald Trump to worry about his neighbors and the people who sent him to Washington.**

---
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| We need your help if we're going to send Chris Pappas packing. [Will you consider donating $15, $25 or even $100 to my campaign](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xNDM3MTU3MzAwMDQ0MzA1NjM0JmM9cTJ1MiZlPTE5MjgmYj0zNTU3MjQ3NTgmZD1iNWk2ZTd4.7mk_uKs7bC-IN5kSY_Zh235IYbP2PikSRonqIfUvluE)?

---
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| The fact is that Chris Pappas is way too liberal for New Hampshire. **He is more focused on getting stimulus checks for illegal immigrants in San Francisco than helping New Hampshire’s small businesses keep afloat during the current crisis.** Now he’s trying to push through the greatest threat to voter integrity that our state has seen with the Democrats’ mail-in voting scheme that he snuck into the CARES Act. We can’t let them take our voice away. We need to fight back. Even worse, just last week he proudly touted the endorsement of several of the Nation’s largest gun-grabbing PACs. With everything that is going on in our country right now, Chris Pappas still doesn’t believe that you have the right to defend yourself and your family. **I am proud to be the only 100% Pro-Second Amendment candidate in this race and I will always defend our right to keep and bear arms.**

| It’s been over 20 years since New Hampshire has sent a Military Veteran to Congress. I am going to break that streak. **The values that I learned in the Air Force are sorely needed in Washington; strength, honor, commitment to community**.
Since I announced my campaign in January, our New Hampshire grassroots support has exploded. **We’ve released over 300 endorsements from local New Hampshire leaders and raised the most in-state money of any candidate in the race. In fact over 94% of our donations have come from New Hampshire.**
Unlike my opponents, I’m not going to spend the next few months recycling the tired talking points from some highly paid Washington DC political consultants. New Hampshire doesn’t need someone from the Swamp to tell us that Chris Pappas has been a disaster. We’ve seen it with our own eyes.**I’m the only candidate in this race that has actually spent more time in New Hampshire than Washington DC. I’ve seen the effects that Pappas’s reckless, partisan policies have taken on the Granite State.** Please join our team. Together we can defeat Chris Pappas and send a conservative, Republican, _New Hampshire_ voice down to Congress! Thank you, Matt

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: NEW: Great American comeback—2.5 million new jobs in May!
body:
Together, President Trump and American workers built the greatest economy on Earth. Now, as we safely reopen from Coronavirus, we are rebuilding... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f9e0a2a8a7&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • June 5, 2020
---
| | |
# **Great American comeback: 2.5 million jobs in May!**

**Together, President Trump** and American workers built the greatest economy on Earth. Now, as we safely reopen from Coronavirus, we are rebuilding stronger than ever!

**“Experts” predicted** that America would lose 7.5 million jobs last month. Instead, our country _added_ 2.5 million—10 million more than expected.

🇺🇸 [President Trump: We’re starting an incredible period of growth!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a3bf7c6892&e=3ee4985eea)

**May’s 2.5 million jobs** are the most new jobs ever recorded in a single month.

**Across our country** , America is coming back. Nearly three-quarters of U.S. small businesses are now open, up from the pandemic low of just 52 percent in April.

**American workers** helped slow the spread of Coronavirus by social distancing and following the guidelines of their local health officials. Many paid a price by losing their jobs to keep the rest of us safe. As we reopen America, they deserve this incredible news.

**All told, last month** our economy added:
* 1.2 MILLION leisure & hospitality jobs

* 464,000 construction jobs

* 424,000 education & healthcare jobs

* 368,000 retail jobs

* 225,000 manufacturing jobs

**“We had the greatest** economy in the history of the world. And that strength let us get through this horrible pandemic,” President Trump said.

**“We’re going to be back** there. I think we’re going to actually be back higher next year than ever before. And the only thing that can stop us is bad policy.”

[WATCH: A record-breaking job surge across American industries!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e6f7a818c2&e=3ee4985eea)

**READ** : [Economy trounces expectations by 10 million jobs](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fcc8bf8262&e=3ee4985eea)

---
|
---
|

name: Joe Biden
subject: re: my announcement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, [www
body:
This will be a massive undertaking, so I need you on board. | |
---
We must vote Donald Trump out of the White House. I know you know this, Derek. But if we're to begin to achieve the transformative change that is required to move our country forward, we need to do much, much more than win just the presidency: **Democrats need to aim to win**** _every_****seat.** From the House and Senate to state legislatures, governorships, and local elections, the change we all seek can't happen unless Democrats can invest and win at every level nationwide. **Today, I'm proudly partnering with the DNC to officially launch the Every Seat Fund.** The Every Seat Fund is our party's commitment to fighting to win important offices not just at the top of the ticket, but at every level and in every single ZIP code. This fight will be a massive undertaking, but it's a fight we must win for ourselves and for future generations. Truth be told, it can't come close to happening without you on board, Derek, which is why I'm coming to you today with a sincere request: **Will you split a $10 contribution between the Every Seat Fund and my campaign? I'm urging you to stay focused on winning not just the Oval Office this November, but organizing for every seat in every community nationwide.**

clean_body: This will be a massive undertaking, so I need you on board. | | We must vote Donald Trump out of the White House. I know you know this, Derek. But if we're to begin to achieve the transformative change that is required to move our country forward, we need to do much, much more than win just the presidency: Democrats need to aim to win everyseat. From the House and Senate to state legislatures, governorships, and local elections, the change we all seek can't happen unless Democrats can invest and win at every level nationwide. Today, I'm proudly partnering with the DNC to officially launch the Every Seat Fund. The Every Seat Fund is our party's commitment to fighting to win important offices not just at the top of the ticket, but at every level and in every single ZIP code. This fight will be a massive undertaking, but it's a fight we must win for ourselves and for future generations. Truth be told, it can't come close to happening without you on board, Derek, which is why I'm coming to you today with a sincere request: Will you split a $10 contribution between the Every Seat Fund and my campaign? I'm urging you to stay focused on winning not just the Oval Office this November, but organizing for every seat in every community nationwide. I've been saying for a long time that this race is about a whole lot more than just me. It's about restoring the soul of our nation and about making it clear that we need our government to work for us, rather than against us. We have the power to cement the values of equality, justice, and compassion into the very foundation of our democracy -- but only if we work at it together. My first job in elected office was as a member of my local county council in Delaware, so I know firsthand what a monumental difference state and local leadership makes in working people's daily lives. The DNC's Every Seat Fund is designed to give candidates up and down the ballot the critical data, organizing, and voter protection resources they'll need, and the DNC is the only party committee responsible for electing Democrats at every level nationwide. Time is running out, Derek, so we must come together to support the fund today. Organizing starts in our communities. Thank you for stepping up. Keep the faith, Joe Joe Biden P.S. The DNC's Every Seat Fund is going to be key to scaling and executing the strategies and programs Democrats have been building together for the last three years. If every person on this grassroots team donated today, our party would be able to fund this work in an instant. But I know that's not feasible for many of us right now -- and that's completely okay.

name: Team Kathy
subject: NEWSLETTER: Updates from Kathy Manning, Democratic Candidate for NC-06
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
| |

[](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d32a/3d199a51/2f4231e8/7364c9bf/1619104803/VEsH/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://www.kathymanningfornc.com/")

|
---
|
---
| |
---
MESSAGE FROM KATHY
---
"This past week, we watched another unarmed black man murdered in broad daylight by white police officers in a frighteningly callous and brutal way. The sheer cruelty in that video was gut wrenching. For so many who have lived with the daily reality of racism, the murder of George Floyd is only the latest manifestation of a history of racism in this country -- racism that has infected law enforcement, housing, healthcare, food security, education, and employment. Too often, too many act as if black lives don't matter. They do." [_**Keep Reading**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d32a/3d199a51/2f4231e8/7364c9bc/1619104803/VEsE/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://medium.com/@info_29140/this-is-a-time-for-leadership-ca598a3e1f59")

| COMMUNITY SERVICE
---
Backpack Beginnings delivers child-centric services to feed, comfort and clothe children in need. One in four children are food insecure in North Carolina. This week, Team Kathy donated food to help this great organization provide kids with food that is especially needed during these tough times. We toured the facility and learned about the meaningful work they do. To support Backpack Beginnings, visit their [_**site**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d32a/3d199a51/2f4231e8/7364c9b7/1619104803/VEsP/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://backpackbeginnings.org/donate/#") and do your part to combat food insecurity in our community.
|
---

| | |
---
| LOCAL ECONOMY
---

Small businesses in Guilford County that are struggling with the economic downturn caused by the coronavirus pandemic can apply for up to $10,000 in aid through a grant program that opened this week. Apply [_**here.**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d32a/3d199a51/2f4231e8/7364c9b4/1619104803/VEsHBQ/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://www.guilfordcountync.gov/services/grants/small-business-grant-application-program")

|
---

| | |
---
| CENSUS 2020
---
The 2020 Census counts every person living in the United States and five U.S. territories and shapes many different aspects of your community. The 2020 Census form is secure, quick, and easy. Voting by mail in North Carolina is easy and available to any registered voter. Learn about how you can apply now for an absentee mail-in ballot for the November election.
* [**_Register to vote_**](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d32a/3d199a51/2f4231e8/7364c9b2/1619104803/VEsHBw/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://www.ncdot.gov/dmv/offices-services/online/Pages/voter-registration-application.aspx")

| COVID TESTING
---
Guilford County Public Health will be offering a number of #COVID-19 community testing opportunities over the next several weeks. Testing will be done BY APPOINTMENT ONLY and MUST be scheduled in advance by calling 336-641-7527. Confidential pre-screening will also take place during the call to confirm a person is a candidate for testing.

body:
I wanted to fill you in on new developments with our campaign from the past week Friend, this week our entire country has been turned upside down again. It’s been an emotional time but we’ve come together in NY-22.
Here are some of the highlights from this week: **We mourned as a nation** Our nation is grieving for the tragic killing of George Floyd caused by the brutality of a bad cop. The depraved acts of all the officers who either committed or stood idly by while a man’s life was unjustly taken must be condemned.
We also must not allow violent rioters to derail the peaceful protests and risk the safety of citizens and law enforcement.
This has gone on long enough. The looting and destruction are causing irreparable harm to businesses on the heels of the Coronavirus pandemic.
New York must deploy all available law enforcement including the National Guard to assist local police and maintain law and order in our communities.
Mr. Floyd’s family is pleading and praying for peace to honor his life.
View and share the post **[here.](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=f43352d8c70a4c4d81e1d3c43f3f2e4f&_e=F0n6nmdtLanNgaHRZQHjvD6aRboUBGW_vuT6jfQH7LX5dvb0w_SFKL55DZAztRl3ZJlXeaR5K0BK_t4EV-XlDntdrSd02EM3JPW4OQE18UK71VBsLWVeLCWOmojf8moFaoe30BaR2TRrt7AuFNyWvlGTxJnr3mrX8IvR7rs7_UtXc_EuLJz5Z_BHmYfSYBm2Pylh_xCNx5aL4Oh2mHkPTeoO3hp9PMlAm9-o7nTqi12ONaJ_63-ULCp8xK6EaPHjxq3jODDWv120CLKOnVp1m2XfzqVIylqSNyv7uM4Q8ApmkKQCheoZ6pSiDD-Z37OKbbwzEijwd8lKK5xI15UbcwLcO_0hXAt7u1Xs0D8Pp8kReerLlkQk6eAKDvu6p-LE_uvQOuYs-QrqJ0zREyxb8fv7JCeZxdlrYOk7E1q9564jlOqHrTZ_8oY4fwHOYIGKrVQhJDWJRpIjLOauvspK65iS3QdO5RyKAGI_yut4hAybygPRoxFoFhYjiDZd3f-Q)** | |

My heart goes out to our brave police officers serving on the front lines of these violent riots. We need decisive leadership that will put an end to the senseless violence.

clean_body: I wanted to fill you in on new developments with our campaign from the past week Friend, this week our entire country has been turned upside down again. It’s been an emotional time but we’ve come together in NY-22. Here are some of the highlights from this week: We mourned as a nation Our nation is grieving for the tragic killing of George Floyd caused by the brutality of a bad cop. The depraved acts of all the officers who either committed or stood idly by while a man’s life was unjustly taken must be condemned. We also must not allow violent rioters to derail the peaceful protests and risk the safety of citizens and law enforcement. This has gone on long enough. The looting and destruction are causing irreparable harm to businesses on the heels of the Coronavirus pandemic. New York must deploy all available law enforcement including the National Guard to assist local police and maintain law and order in our communities. Mr. Floyd’s family is pleading and praying for peace to honor his life. View and share the post | | Met with our NY-22 farmers! I held a Zoom roundtable with farmers across #NY22 to discuss the various issues they are facing. Family farms are the bedrock of our Upstate economy and I will continue to tirelessly advocate for our hardworking agriculture community! View and share the post | Celebrated the support of President Trump I am honored to have President Trump's endorsement. Please remember to vote in the Republican Primary on June 23rd. Lets win #NY22 and Take Back the House! View and share the post | Called out Brindisi’s deafening silence Anthony Brindisi’s silence speaks volumes. Join me at to see exactly how long he’s been silent and sign our petition to hold him accountable for abandoning elderly New Yorkers. View and share the video | Stood up for local business If violent rioters can loot stores, assault police and defame public property, our Upstate small businesses can safely re-open. We have taken the necessary precautions to flatten the curve. Re-open New York! View and share the post | WE BACK THE BLUE My heart goes out to our brave police officers serving on the front lines of these violent riots. Bad actors are trampling the legacy of George Floyd by looting, burning and now assaulting law enforcement. City and State officials cannot let this go on any longer. We need decisive leadership that will put an end to the senseless violence. View and share the post | Hosted a Zoom townhall with the Southern Tier Thank you everyone who joined our Southern Tier Zoom Town Hall. It was great to hear all of your comments and concerns. name: Tom Cotton
subject: the Liberal mob WON
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate
body:
We knew this was coming. After the New York Times published my opinion piece promoting law and order--liberal reporters and woke progressives threw a major temper tantrum. The New York Times has totally caved to these radicals and is now apologizing for publishing my piece. This started with the brutal and wrongful death of George Floyd. And then peaceful protests hijacked by mass unrest, resulting in more death and destruction.
Click here to [unsubscribe](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42669597&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
Peter,

We knew this was coming. After the New York Times published my opinion piece promoting law and order--liberal reporters and woke progressives threw a major temper tantrum.

**The New York Times has totally caved to these radicals and is now apologizing for publishing my piece.**

This started with the brutal and wrongful death of George Floyd.

And then peaceful protests hijacked by mass unrest, resulting in more death and destruction.

There's no debate on protecting the right to peacefully protest. But our society needs to be able to freely debate how to respond to the violence and the riots.

That's what I did, in seriousness and good faith, by publishing my perspective with the New York Times.

Interestingly, take a look at a few other editorials the New York Times has published without a second thought.

[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=4&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=2&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

[****](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=14&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
[**Peter, if you 've had enough of these politically correct liberals at the New York Times join my Stand for Freedom fund by making a donation of $10, $25 or even $50.**](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=14&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
_****_
[STAND FOR FREEDOM](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=12&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---

Today, the New York Times is hosting a town hall meeting and reporters are planning a virtual walkout! They have totally put journalistic integrity aside and are instead allowing the liberal mob to win.

My view is not controversial. If police are overwhelmed by violence, military backup is a lawful & appropriate last resort with historical precedent.

In fact, 58% of Americans in recent polling support my position of returning law and order to our streets.

The liberal mob is already attacking me and it is only just the beginning. [**I am counting on you to join me and Stand for Freedom by making a donation of $5, $10, or even $25.**](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=6&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
_****_
[STAND FOR FREEDOM](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=9&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---

In service,

Tom Cotton
[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI2Njk1OTcS1&kn=5&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MDQ1ODk5NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Simona=20Grace?=
subject: It's Because of You!
body:
May was our strongest grassroots fundraising month in 2020. We are small PAC that operates on a large scale across, helping candidates across the US. | | | |
---
|
# We are able to stretch every dollar
---
| [ ](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=a2370f2d8a&e=3f151f8b74)
---
**Moms in Office is committed to making sure that most of our fundraising dollars are donated directly to the campaigns of our endorsed candidates. **
| | | [Chip in](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=a7767af0ad&e=3f151f8b74 "Chip in")
---
|
###
I am so grateful to you-

In the month of May, we have endorsed several women who are running for office across the country and thanks to supporters like you, we are going to announce our next slate of endorsements in the coming days.

This would not have been possible without the hundreds of grassroots donations we have received just in the month of May.

This would not have been possible without the moms who run this organization with me across the country, literally from their kitchen counter. Moms in Office is a small political organization that operates on an incredibly large scale because we are one of the only organizations in the country dedicated to supporting moms running for office.

We are the only organization that guarantees a financial contribution with every endorsement because we are volunteer-run by a coalition of moms in politics. All of us have made a commitment to elect changemakers by changing the way how endorsements are made in the first place.

We truly live our values and back candidates based on the strength of their conviction, not their connections to corporate America.

**I want to share something personal with you. **

I grew up in a communist country in the countryside of Hungary under one roof with my great-grandmother, my grandparents, my sister, and my mom. I never imagined that the little girl who used to get oranges for Christmas would one day have the opportunity to study abroad, graduate from UCLA, summa cum laude, and live in the greatest country of the world; America.

My life may look different today, but when it comes to my values, I am grounded in that same experience of gratitude that I felt when my family was able to afford to buy oranges once a year.

**I know the value of a dollar. **

This is why I am grateful to you for making a contribution to us in any amount.

**If every person who receives this email today would donate [$5](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=ece9767de9&e=3f151f8b74) we would be able to fund our budget for a year.

If every person who receives this email today would give just [$1](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=cbdec4e2ac&e=3f151f8b74) we would be able to contribute $5,000 to the campaigns of at least 10 moms.

If we received[ $10 ](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=9f579c6b70&e=3f151f8b74)from just a few hundred people who receive this email today, we would be able to fund our next slate of endorsements and provide the max contribution to each candidate.**

Thank you for your continued support!

With Gratitude,

Simona Grace
Founder, Moms in Office

---
| [Chip in! ](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=c2638ea706&e=3f151f8b74 "Chip in! ")
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
**Simona Grace's quest to shape the world started at a young age.
[LA PARENT MAGAZINE](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=ca69d5d869&e=3f151f8b74)**
| | | | | | | | [](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=1a548f7388&e=3f151f8b74)
---
| | [](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=f299302f21&e=3f151f8b74)
---
| | [](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=019538d19a&e=3f151f8b74)
---
| | [](www.momsinoffice.org)
---
|
---

You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Moms in Office13351 Riverside Dr # 265Sherman Oaks, CA 91423-2542
[Add us to your address book](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/vcard?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=5b1ae3321e)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/profile?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=5b1ae3321e&e=3f151f8b74) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=5b1ae3321e&e=3f151f8b74&c=7a31899131).
